title: Brain Implants Market Size to Reach USD 18.39 Billion by 2035, Growing at 9.99% CAGR | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global brain implants market, valued at USD 7.10 billion in 2025, is projected to reach USD 18.39 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 9.99%. Growth is driven by rising prevalence of neurological disorders and increasing adoption of deep brain stimulation, neuromodulation systems, and brain-computer interfaces. North America leads the market, with the U.S. expected to grow from USD 3.16 billion to USD 8.17 billion by 2035.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a leading market player and received FDA clearance in 2024 for the Percept RC rechargeable deep brain stimulation system with advanced closed-loop stimulation technology, demonstrating strong innovation and market presence.
Listed as a leading market player through its St. Jude Medical subsidiary, which is a major neuromodulation company benefiting from the expanding brain implants market.
Listed as a leading market player in the rapidly expanding brain implants market with strong positioning in neuromodulation technologies.
Listed as a leading market player with exposure to the growing vagus nerve stimulation segment, which is projected to register the fastest growth during the forecast period.

title: Microcatheter Market Size to Reach USD 1.79 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global microcatheter market is valued at USD 1.05 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 1.79 billion by 2035, expanding at a CAGR of 5.53%. Growth is driven by increasing adoption of minimally invasive cardiovascular and neurovascular procedures, rising prevalence of cardiovascular and neurovascular disorders, and advancements in catheter technologies. Aspiration microcatheters lead with 36% market share, while steerable microcatheters are the fastest-growing segment. North America dominates with 34% of global revenues, with Asia-Pacific emerging as the fastest-growing region.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a key player in the growing microcatheter market with above-market per-unit commercial value in North America's advanced interventional cardiology and neurointerventional radiology communities.

Highlighted as a major market player and specifically noted for launching the Solitaire X revascularization device with Phenom 27 microcatheter system in 2024, demonstrating active innovation and market participation.
Identified as a leading player in the microcatheter market with premium device specifications and above-market commercial value in North America's interventional cardiology sector.

Recognized as a key market player and specifically noted for launching the 3D REVASCULARIZATION catheter system in 2024, demonstrating innovation in aspiration microcatheter technology for stroke treatment.

Listed as a significant player in the microcatheter market with premium device specifications and commercial operations in North America's interventional cardiology and neurointerventional radiology sectors.

title: Meet the Medtech Stock Wall Street Thinks Will Soar 65% Over the Next 12 Months
author: Thomas Niel
description: Boston Scientific shares have plummeted over 50% year-to-date due to repeated downward guidance revisions, particularly regarding declining Watchman procedure usage. Despite bearish market sentiment, 27 of 31 analysts maintain buy ratings with a consensus price target of $78 (65% upside), though the article cautions this recovery may be challenging without faster-than-expected growth acceleration.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has fallen over 50% YTD with management repeatedly walking back growth guidance. CEO cited declining Watchman procedure usage and expects flat dollar growth in Q2 and Q3. While analysts remain relatively upbeat, the article warns that achieving the $78 price target consensus may prove challenging without faster-than-expected recovery.
Mentioned only as a comparable medical device company trading at similar forward valuations to Boston Scientific. No specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.

title: Peripheral Vascular Devices Market by Type, End User - Global Forecast to 2031
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global peripheral vascular devices market is projected to grow from USD 10.02 billion in 2026 to USD 13.22 billion by 2031 at a CAGR of 5.7%, driven by rising prevalence of vascular disorders, aging populations, and innovations in minimally invasive devices. Asia Pacific is expected to see the highest growth, with major players including Boston Scientific, Medtronic, and Abbott leading the market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major player in the growing peripheral vascular devices market, which is expanding at 5.7% CAGR with strong demand drivers including aging populations and innovations in minimally invasive devices.
Identified as a key industry player in the peripheral vascular devices market experiencing significant growth driven by rising prevalence of vascular disorders and favorable reimbursement policies.
Named among major players in the expanding peripheral vascular devices market, benefiting from increased adoption of endovascular devices and growing awareness of early diagnosis and treatment.

title: Zscaler, AutoZone, And Regencell Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (May 25-May 29): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks experienced significant declines during the week of May 25-29, 2026. Zscaler led losses with a 20.06% drop following disappointing Q3 results and weak Q4 guidance. Other major losers included Boston Scientific (16.95%), AutoZone (14.21%), Regencell Bioscience (12.61%), and Venture Global (10.87%). Declines were attributed to earnings misses, analyst downgrades, and energy sector weakness following geopolitical developments.
